title: 10 possible side effects of Saussurea lappa  (costus)

side_effects_list.side_effect_desc: Saussurea lappa irritates skin causing rashes. This is attributed to its volatile oils and sesquiterpenes, which can cause an allergic reaction or irritation when used topically. The irritating compounds may bind to skin proteins, triggering an immune response and leading to the development of rashes. As a result, individuals who are sensitive to Saussurea lappa's active ingredients may experience this adverse effect.

Saussurea lappa induces vomiting in overdose due to its emetic properties. When taken in excess, the plant's saponins and sesquiterpenes can stimulate the gag reflex, leading to intense nausea and vomiting. This is a common adverse effect associated with the overuse or misuse of Saussurea lappa, which can be potentially harmful if not monitored closely.

Saussurea lappa causes stomach upset easily due to its bitter and astringent properties, which can irritate the mucous membranes in the digestive tract. The presence of saponins and sesquiterpenes in this herb may also contribute to this side effect by altering gut bacteria balance and increasing gut motility, leading to stomach discomfort and nausea.

Saussurea lappa lowers blood pressure significantly. This is attributed to its ability to relax the smooth muscle in blood vessels, leading to a decrease in blood vessel tone and subsequent reduction in blood pressure. Additionally, the herb's beta-blocking properties may also contribute to this effect by inhibiting the effects of epinephrine, a hormone that increases heart rate and blood pressure. As a result, individuals using Saussurea lappa may experience decreased blood pressure levels.

Saussurea lappa increases urine output suddenly due to its diuretic properties. The bioactive compounds present in Saussurea lappa, such as sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, stimulate the kidneys to produce more urine, leading to increased excretion of fluids. This effect is likely attributed to the plant's ability to inhibit sodium reabsorption in the kidneys, resulting in a sudden increase in urine production.

Saussurea lappa disrupts menstrual cycles irregularly due to its ability to influence hormonal balances in the body. The root and rhizome of this plant are rich in estrogenic compounds, which can stimulate or suppress ovulation, leading to irregular periods. Additionally, Saussurea lappa's phytochemical makeup can also affect progesterone levels, further disrupting menstrual cycles.

Saussurea lappa interacts with anticoagulants negatively. This may cause an increased risk of bleeding due to its ability to thin the blood and enhance platelet aggregation inhibition. The plant's bioactive compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, can potentiate the effects of anticoagulant medications, leading to adverse outcomes in individuals taking these medications concurrently.

Saussurea lappa affects heart rate noticeably slow due to its ability to stimulate the parasympathetic nervous system. This can cause a decrease in cardiac output and peripheral resistance, leading to a slower heart rate. The cardiotonic compounds present in Saussurea lappa, such as costunolide and dehydrocostus lactone, are thought to be responsible for this effect. As the heart rate slows down, it may lead to feelings of calmness and relaxation, although further research is needed to fully understand the mechanisms behind this interaction.

Saussurea lappa triggers allergic reactions strongly. This is due to its high content of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds, which can stimulate the immune system and cause an overactive response. As a result, individuals may experience symptoms such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing when exposed to Saussurea lappa, particularly if they have a history of allergies or are sensitive to certain plant compounds.

Saussurea lappa enhances bleeding risk greatly due to its potent antiplatelet and anticoagulant properties, which may interfere with blood clotting. The herb's ability to inhibit platelet aggregation and increase levels of pro-inflammatory eicosanoids can lead to increased bleeding tendency, particularly in individuals who are already taking medications that affect blood coagulation or have a history of bleeding disorders. This side effect can be especially concerning for those undergoing surgical procedures or experiencing traumatic injuries.

intro: Saussurea lappa has some side effects when used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset. These side effects can occur due to the herb's high concentration of volatile oils, which can irritate the digestive system and cause discomfort. If left untreated, these side effects can worsen, leading to decreased appetite, fatigue, and even malnutrition, significantly impacting daily life and overall well-being.
intro_desc: Saussurea lappa has some side effects when used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. These side effects can be caused by taking high doses of the herb. For instance, consuming large amounts can lead to stomach cramps, bloating, and abdominal pain, making it difficult for individuals to perform daily activities, work, or maintain a healthy lifestyle.
